scenarios.titleEn: Witnessed collapse at a gym (adult VF arrest)
scenarios.titleZh: 健身房目击倒地（成人 VF 心脏骤停）
scenarios.settingEn: A 54-year-old male suddenly collapses on a treadmill at a busy gym. Staff saw him clutch his chest, stumble, and fall. A bystander started compressions before your arrival.
scenarios.settingZh: 54 岁男性在健身房跑步机上突然倒地。工作人员看到他先捂胸、踉跄，然后倒下。你到场前已有旁观者开始按压。
scenarios.dispatchEn: Unconscious, not breathing normally, CPR in progress, AED available on site.
scenarios.dispatchZh: 无意识、无正常呼吸、现场 CPR 进行中，现场有 AED。
arrivalFindingsEn: Patient supine on the floor, unresponsive, apneic except for occasional agonal gasps
No carotid pulse felt within 10 seconds
Skin pale and cool, pupils mid-sized
Gym staff report collapse was less than 4 minutes ago
arrivalFindingsZh: 患者平躺地面，无反应，除偶发濒死喘息外无正常呼吸
10 秒内摸不到颈动脉搏动
皮肤苍白偏凉，瞳孔中等大小
健身房人员表示倒地发生不到 4 分钟
cprFocusEn: Recognize agonal respirations are not normal breathing
High-quality CPR with rapid defibrillation priority
Minimize peri-shock pause and rotate compressors early
Use pit-crew style role assignment if enough helpers are present
cprFocusZh: 识别濒死喘息不等于正常呼吸
高质量 CPR + 尽快除颤是核心
尽量减少电击前后暂停时间，尽早轮换按压者
若现场人手足够，尽量用 pit-crew 分工
examPearlsEn: Classic license-style arrest: witnessed adult collapse + immediate CPR + shockable rhythm
Strong candidates think about pad placement, compressor rotation, and scene leadership
Do not waste time on long pulse checks or unnecessary movement before first shock
examPearlsZh: 这是典型 license exam 心脏骤停题：目击倒地 + 立即 CPR + 可电击节律
高分点常在贴片位置、按压轮换、现场领导与节奏控制
不要在首次电击前浪费时间做过长脉搏检查或无意义搬动

scenarios.titleZh: 家中未目击心脏骤停（老年人，停搏时间不明）
scenarios.settingEn: An 81-year-old female is found on the bedroom floor by her husband at 06:30. He last saw her awake around 02:00 when she went to the bathroom.
scenarios.settingZh: 81 岁女性在卧室地板上被丈夫于早上 06:30 发现。丈夫最后一次看到她清醒是在凌晨 02:00，她起床去洗手间时。
scenarios.dispatchEn: Cardiac or respiratory arrest, downtime unknown.
scenarios.dispatchZh: 心脏或呼吸骤停，停搏时间不明。
arrivalFindingsEn: Patient unresponsive, not breathing, pulseless
Dependent lividity is not obvious, body cool but not cold
Medication bottles nearby include antihypertensives and anticoagulants
Family is distraught and asking if she can still be saved
arrivalFindingsZh: 患者无反应、无呼吸、无脉搏
尸斑不明显，身体偏凉但不冰冷
附近药瓶包括降压药和抗凝药
家属非常崩溃，不断问还有没有救
cprFocusEn: Work an organized arrest while also gathering downtime clues
Balance immediate resuscitation with recognition of prognosis and context
Maintain compression quality even in emotionally difficult home scenes
Scene communication and family management matter
cprFocusZh: 一边有条理地进行心脏骤停处理，一边收集停搏时间线索
要在立即复苏与预后/情境判断之间取得平衡
即使在情绪化家庭现场，也要保持按压质量
现场沟通与家属管理同样重要
examPearlsEn: This tests judgment under uncertainty, not just compression mechanics
Candidates must think about downtime clues, medications, and reversible causes
The emotional scene is part of the exam difficulty
examPearlsZh: 这类题考的是不确定情境下的判断，不只是按压动作
考生要想到停搏时间线索、药物背景和可逆病因
家庭情绪现场本身就是考试难点的一部分

scenarios.titleEn: Restaurant choking that progresses to arrest
scenarios.titleZh: 餐厅异物梗阻进展为心脏骤停
scenarios.settingEn: A 67-year-old male choked on steak in a restaurant. Initially he was conscious and unable to speak; by the time you arrive he has collapsed.
scenarios.settingZh: 67 岁男性在餐厅吃牛排时发生气道异物梗阻。最初他清醒但无法说话；等你到场时已经倒地。
scenarios.dispatchZh: 异物梗阻，现已失去意识。
arrivalFindingsEn: Patient is cyanotic, pulseless, and apneic
Bystanders attempted abdominal thrusts before collapse
Food debris is seen in the mouth but not easily removable with fingers
Family states collapse happened less than 2 minutes ago
arrivalFindingsZh: 患者发绀、无脉搏、无呼吸
旁观者在倒地前做过腹部冲击
口腔内可见食物残渣，但无法轻易手指清除
家属称倒地发生在 2 分钟内
cprFocusEn: Begin CPR immediately once unresponsive and pulseless
Think about airway obstruction as the likely cause of arrest
Coordinate compressions with airway attempts and visible foreign body checks
Avoid blind finger sweeps
cprFocusZh: 一旦无反应无脉搏，立刻开始 CPR
要把气道异物梗阻视为最可能的骤停原因
将按压、气道处理和可见异物检查有节奏地配合起来
避免盲目手指掏取
examPearlsEn: This is a classic airway-to-arrest transition scenario
The exam often checks whether you change from choking algorithm to full arrest management at the right time
Good candidates remember that oxygenation/ventilation problems caused the arrest
examPearlsZh: 这是典型“异物梗阻转心脏骤停”场景
考试常考你是否能在正确时间点从 choking 流程切到 full arrest management
高分考生会记得：本质上是氧合/通气问题导致的骤停

scenarios.titleEn: Pediatric drowning arrest at a backyard pool
scenarios.titleZh: 后院泳池儿童溺水心脏骤停
scenarios.settingEn: A 4-year-old child is pulled from a backyard pool during a family gathering. Estimated submersion time is 3 to 5 minutes before removal from the water.
scenarios.settingZh: 家庭聚会时，一名 4 岁儿童从后院泳池中被拉出。估计淹没时间约 3 到 5 分钟。
arrivalFindingsEn: Child is limp, cyanotic, and unresponsive
No palpable pulse, no effective respirations
Wet clothing, pool deck crowded with panicked family members
One family member is attempting shallow ineffective compressions
arrivalFindingsZh: 儿童身体松软、发绀、无反应
摸不到脉搏，无有效呼吸
衣物湿透，泳池边围满惊慌家属
一位家属正在做很浅且无效的按压
cprFocusEn: Pediatric arrest is often hypoxic in origin, so oxygenation and ventilation matter heavily
Deliver high-quality age-appropriate CPR with effective ventilations
Control scene chaos and create working space rapidly
Anticipate the emotional weight of a pediatric arrest
cprFocusZh: 儿科骤停常由缺氧引起，所以氧合与通气特别关键
要做符合年龄的高质量 CPR，并确保有效通气
快速控制混乱现场，腾出工作空间
要预判儿科骤停带来的巨大情绪压力
examPearlsEn: This tests whether you remember that many pediatric arrests are respiratory first, cardiac second
Candidates are expected to adapt CPR technique to child size
Scene control and leadership are often embedded in the stem
examPearlsZh: 这类题常考：儿科骤停往往先是呼吸问题，后才发展成心脏骤停
考生需要根据儿童体型调整 CPR 技术
题干里常暗含现场控制和领导能力考点

scenarios.titleEn: Unresponsive infant in a crib (possible SIDS presentation)
scenarios.titleZh: 婴儿床内无反应婴儿（疑似 SIDS 表现）
scenarios.settingEn: A 3-month-old infant is found unresponsive in a crib by a parent during an afternoon nap check. The infant was last seen normal 45 minutes earlier.
scenarios.settingZh: 家长午睡查看时发现一名 3 个月大的婴儿在婴儿床中无反应。45 分钟前最后一次看到时仍正常。
arrivalFindingsEn: Infant is pale, limp, and apneic
No central pulse appreciated quickly
Room is quiet but emotionally overwhelming, parents crying and blaming themselves
No obvious trauma or signs of choking
arrivalFindingsZh: 婴儿苍白、松软、无呼吸
无法迅速摸到中心脉搏
房间很安静，但情绪极度沉重，父母在哭并自责
无明显外伤或异物梗阻迹象
cprFocusEn: Immediate infant CPR with correct technique and ratio awareness
Gentle but efficient airway positioning and ventilations
Maintain professionalism despite intense parental distress
Document timeline carefully because history matters
cprFocusZh: 立刻按正确婴儿技术开始 CPR，并注意按压通气比例
气道摆位和通气要轻柔但高效
在家长极度悲痛的情况下依然保持专业
仔细记录时间线，因为病史对判断很重要
examPearlsEn: Infant scenarios test fine motor CPR details and emotional control
The exam may probe whether you can stay systematic in a devastating scene
History and timing remain important even when CPR is underway
examPearlsZh: 婴儿场景常考 CPR 细节动作和情绪稳定度
考试也会看你能否在极端悲剧现场保持系统性
即使 CPR 已开始，病史和时间线仍然重要

scenarios.titleEn: Opioid overdose arrest in a public washroom
scenarios.titleZh: 公共洗手间阿片类药物过量致心脏骤停
scenarios.settingEn: A 29-year-old male is found collapsed in a locked mall washroom stall. Security forced entry after he had been inside for an unknown amount of time.
scenarios.settingZh: 29 岁男性被发现倒在商场洗手间隔间内。保安在其停留时间不明的情况下强行开门进入。
scenarios.dispatchZh: 无意识患者，疑似药物过量。
arrivalFindingsEn: Patient unresponsive, cyanotic, pulseless, not breathing
Drug paraphernalia nearby including burnt foil and syringe
Scene is cramped and awkward for team positioning
Friends on scene say he used fentanyl 10 minutes ago
arrivalFindingsZh: 患者无反应、发绀、无脉搏、无呼吸
旁边有吸毒工具，包括烧焦锡纸和注射器
现场狭窄，团队站位和操作都困难
同行朋友说他 10 分钟前吸食了 fentanyl
cprFocusEn: Treat as cardiac arrest when pulseless, not just overdose sedation
High-quality CPR in a confined environment requires deliberate positioning
Address airway/ventilation aggressively because the arrest is likely hypoxic/toxicologic in origin
Scene safety and sharps awareness matter
cprFocusZh: 既然无脉搏，就按心脏骤停处理，而不只是药物过量昏睡
狭小空间里的高质量 CPR 需要刻意设计站位
由于很可能是缺氧/毒理性骤停，气道与通气处理尤其关键
要兼顾现场安全和锐器风险
examPearlsEn: This tests whether you can correctly transition from overdose thinking to arrest thinking
Candidates must not let the cause distract them from basic arrest priorities
Confined-space logistics are often part of the challenge
examPearlsZh: 这类题考你能否从 overdose 思维正确切换到 arrest 思维
不能因为病因明显，就忽略基础的骤停优先级
狭小环境操作常是题目附加难点

scenarios.titleZh: 高速车祸创伤性心脏骤停
scenarios.settingEn: A 36-year-old unrestrained driver is ejected during a high-speed rollover on a highway. Fire has already secured the vehicle hazards when you arrive.
scenarios.settingZh: 36 岁未系安全带司机在高速翻滚车祸中被甩出车外。你到场时消防已控制车辆相关危险。
scenarios.dispatchEn: Major trauma, patient ejected, CPR may be needed.
scenarios.dispatchZh: 重大创伤，患者被甩出，可能需要 CPR。
arrivalFindingsEn: Patient supine on gravel shoulder, apneic and pulseless
Massive facial trauma, chest wall deformity, and suspected pelvic instability
Bystanders attempted to move the patient before EMS arrival
Blood visible on clothing and roadway
arrivalFindingsZh: 患者仰卧在路肩碎石地面，无呼吸无脉搏
面部重创、胸壁畸形、疑似骨盆不稳
旁观者在 EMS 到达前试图移动患者
衣物和路面可见血迹
cprFocusEn: Traumatic arrest requires thinking beyond compressions alone
Address likely reversible causes such as airway compromise, tension physiology, and exsanguination context
Manual stabilization and team choreography matter when trauma and CPR collide
Avoid treating this like a simple medical VF arrest
cprFocusZh: 创伤性骤停不能只想到按压本身
要同时考虑可逆原因，如气道问题、张力生理、失血背景
当创伤处理和 CPR 叠加时，手动固定与团队编排很重要
不能把这题当成普通医疗性 VF 骤停来做
examPearlsEn: A license exam may use this to test distinction between medical and traumatic arrest thinking
Strong candidates identify mechanism, prognosis clues, and reversible priorities quickly
Scene safety and trauma pattern recognition remain essential
examPearlsZh: license exam 常用这类题考“医疗性骤停”和“创伤性骤停”思路区别
高分点在快速识别损伤机制、预后线索和可逆优先项
现场安全和创伤模式识别仍是核心

scenarios.titleEn: Postpartum collapse at home with CPR in progress
scenarios.titleZh: 产后居家突然倒地，现场 CPR 进行中
scenarios.settingEn: A 32-year-old female is 6 days postpartum and suddenly collapses at home after complaining of shortness of breath and chest pain.
scenarios.settingZh: 32 岁女性产后第 6 天，在主诉呼吸困难和胸痛后于家中突然倒地。
scenarios.dispatchEn: Female unconscious, not breathing, family doing CPR.
scenarios.dispatchZh: 女性无意识、无呼吸，家属正在做 CPR。
arrivalFindingsEn: Patient is pulseless and apneic on the living room floor
Husband states she looked anxious and breathless for 10 minutes before collapse
Fresh postpartum supplies and medications are visible nearby
A newborn is crying in the room, increasing family chaos
arrivalFindingsZh: 患者躺在客厅地板上，无脉搏无呼吸
丈夫说她倒地前约 10 分钟就已经焦躁、气短
附近可见产后用品和药物
房内新生儿正在哭，进一步加剧家庭混乱
cprFocusEn: Run high-quality CPR while considering postpartum high-risk causes such as embolic events
Recognize pre-arrest dyspnea/chest pain as a major clue
Family and environmental distraction can degrade performance if not controlled
This is a high-acuity medical arrest with a specific risk context
cprFocusZh: 进行高质量 CPR 的同时，要考虑产后高风险病因，如栓塞事件
要把倒地前的气短和胸痛视为重要线索
如果不控制家庭和环境干扰，操作质量很容易下降
这是带有特定高风险背景的高危医疗性骤停
examPearlsEn: The stem is testing whether you recognize postpartum red flags before and during arrest management
This is not just a generic adult collapse
Context can change your differential dramatically
examPearlsZh: 题干是在考你是否能在骤停处理前后都识别产后红旗
这不是普通成人倒地题
背景信息会显著改变你的鉴别思路

scenarios.titleZh: 透析后在透析中心突发心脏骤停
scenarios.settingEn: A 63-year-old male collapses in a dialysis clinic waiting area just after completing his treatment.
scenarios.settingZh: 63 岁男性在刚完成透析后，于透析中心等待区突然倒地。
scenarios.dispatchEn: Patient collapsed, CPR started by clinic staff.
scenarios.dispatchZh: 患者倒地，诊所人员已开始 CPR。
arrivalFindingsEn: Patient unresponsive, apneic, pulseless
Dialysis access is present in the arm
Clinic staff report he complained of weakness and palpitations before collapse
Monitor hookup is delayed because the scene is cluttered with chairs and equipment
arrivalFindingsZh: 患者无反应、无呼吸、无脉搏
手臂可见透析通路
诊所人员表示他倒地前主诉无力和心悸
现场椅子和器械杂乱，导致监护连接稍有延误
cprFocusEn: Think about dialysis-related reversible causes while maintaining CPR basics
Avoid letting equipment clutter delay compressions or defibrillation readiness
Use history to guide likely cause without slowing arrest care
Team coordination in semi-clinical environments matters
cprFocusZh: 在保持 CPR 基础优先级的同时，考虑透析相关可逆病因
不要让器械杂乱耽误按压和除颤准备
要用病史帮助判断病因，但不能拖慢骤停处理
半临床环境下的团队协调同样重要
examPearlsEn: This kind of question often points toward electrolyte-driven arrest thinking
Candidates should integrate context fast without freezing on pathophysiology
The exam rewards practical priorities, not over-analysis
examPearlsZh: 这类题常把你往电解质相关骤停思路上带
考生要快速整合背景，而不是卡死在病理生理分析里
考试更奖励实用优先级，不是过度分析

scenarios.titleEn: Lightning strike with sudden collapse in a sports field
scenarios.titleZh: 运动场雷击后突然心脏骤停
scenarios.settingEn: During a storm delay at an outdoor soccer field, a 17-year-old player is struck by lightning and collapses immediately.
scenarios.settingZh: 户外足球场因暴风雨暂停比赛时，一名 17 岁球员遭雷击后立即倒地。
arrivalFindingsEn: Patient is unresponsive and apneic with no pulse
Clothing is partially burned, nearby players are screaming and scattered
The storm is moving away but scene safety still needs confirmation
Coaches state the collapse was immediate and witnessed
arrivalFindingsZh: 患者无反应、无呼吸、无脉搏
衣物部分烧灼，周围球员惊叫四散
雷暴正在远离，但现场安全仍需确认
教练表示是目击性立即倒地
cprFocusEn: Scene safety first, then rapid CPR once safe to enter
This is a special mechanism but still an arrest requiring disciplined basics
Because it is witnessed and sudden, organized rapid intervention matters
Do not be distracted by the dramatic mechanism alone
cprFocusZh: 先确认现场安全，再迅速进入开始 CPR
这虽然是特殊机制，但本质上仍是需要扎实基础处理的骤停
由于是目击性突然倒地，快速而有组织的介入很关键
不要只被戏剧性机制吸走注意力
examPearlsEn: Exams like unusual mechanisms to see whether you still prioritize scene safety and basic arrest care
Witnessed sudden collapse means early intervention may matter greatly
Mechanism is important, but priorities remain recognizable
examPearlsZh: 考试喜欢用这种少见机制，看你会不会仍然守住现场安全和基础骤停处理
目击性突然倒地意味着早期干预可能非常关键
机制特殊，但优先级依然是可识别的

scenarios.titleEn: Nursing home arrest with conflicting family information
scenarios.titleZh: 养老院心脏骤停并伴随家属信息冲突
scenarios.settingEn: A 78-year-old male in a nursing facility is found pulseless in bed shortly after staff heard a thud. Staff started CPR and report he is listed as full code, but one family member arriving on scene insists he never wanted this.
scenarios.settingZh: 78 岁男性在养老机构床上被发现无脉搏。工作人员听到响动后发现其倒地并开始 CPR。院方称他登记为 full code，但赶到现场的一位家属坚称他从不想被这样抢救。
scenarios.dispatchEn: Cardiac arrest in care home, CPR in progress.
scenarios.dispatchZh: 养老院心脏骤停，现场 CPR 进行中。
arrivalFindingsEn: Patient pulseless, apneic, CPR underway by staff
Medication list includes CHF, COPD, atrial fibrillation, and dementia history
Printed paperwork is disorganized and not immediately clear
Family conflict begins while your team is trying to take over resuscitation
arrivalFindingsZh: 患者无脉搏、无呼吸，工作人员正在 CPR
药物清单提示 CHF、COPD、房颤和失智病史
纸本文件杂乱，短时间内无法立即看清
当你团队准备接手复苏时，家属冲突已经开始
cprFocusEn: Take over CPR efficiently while sorting code-status documentation appropriately
Do not let verbal conflict alone derail immediate resuscitation if paperwork is unclear
Scene leadership and communication are central to this scenario
Clinical arrest care and ethical communication are both being examined
cprFocusZh: 高效接手 CPR，同时妥善厘清 code status 文件
如果正式文件不清，不要因为口头冲突就立刻偏离当前复苏流程
现场领导和沟通是这个场景的核心
这题同时在考临床骤停处理和伦理沟通
examPearlsEn: License exams often mix CPR skills with legal/ethical ambiguity
The challenge is staying calm, evidence-based, and organized
Candidates should recognize that documentation status matters more than shouting
examPearlsZh: license exam 很爱把 CPR 技能和法律/伦理模糊点混在一起考
难点在于你能否保持冷静、讲证据、讲秩序
考生应意识到：正式文件状态通常比现场喊叫更重要
